name: Barranco del Rey from Funes via the Rio Arga
name_original: Barranco del Rey desde Funes por el Rio Arga
region_intro: Hiking around Funes offers diverse trails through varied landscapes. The region features paths along rivers like the Rio Arga and through areas such as Barranco del Rey. Hikers can expect routes that traverse open terrain and potentially some forested sections, with moderate elevation changes. The trail network provides options for different skill levels, from gentle riverside walks to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Funes The most popular hiking route is Barranco del Rey – circular desde Funes, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barranco del Rey from Funes via the Rio Arga, an easy 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path. This trail follows the Rio Arga, providing riverside scenery. Local hikers also love the Ruta del Visón Trail, a 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Funes is defined by river paths, open landscapes, and moderate elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Funes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Funes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Funes?
A: There are over 50 hiking routes in the Funes area, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find paths ranging from easy riverside walks to more challenging routes with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or families in Funes?
A: Yes, Funes offers many easy hiking options. Approximately two-thirds of the trails are classified as easy. An excellent choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970779767/barranco-del-rey-from-funes-via-the-rio-arga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Rey from Funes via the Rio Arga&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.0-mile (6.4 km) path that follows the scenic Rio Arga.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Funes hiking trails?
A: Hiking around Funes is characterized by diverse terrain, including river paths, open landscapes, and some forested sections. You can expect moderate elevation changes on many routes, providing varied scenery throughout your hike.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Funes?
A: Yes, Funes features several circular routes perfect for exploring the local landscape. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103051433/barranco-del-rey-circular-desde-funes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Rey – circular desde Funes&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.3-mile (11.8 km) trail that offers a complete loop through the area.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Funes?
A: The hiking trails in Funes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic river paths, and the well-maintained network of trails that cater to different abilities.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or places to see near the hiking trails in Funes?
A: Yes, there are several notable attractions nearby. You could visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5259467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, or see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Collegiate Church&lt;/a&gt;. These offer cultural insights and historical beauty to complement your outdoor adventures.

Q: What is a moderately challenging hike I can take in Funes?
A: For a moderately challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1106426361/ruta-del-vison-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Visón Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This 8.7-mile (14.0 km) route leads through varied terrain and typically takes around 3 hours and 33 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or offer waterside views?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Funes feature river paths, providing beautiful waterside scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970779767/barranco-del-rey-from-funes-via-the-rio-arga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Rey from Funes via the Rio Arga&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a pleasant walk alongside the Rio Arga.

Q: What is the longest hike available in Funes?
A: One of the longer options is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1112818825/las-boyas-del-campo-a-rio-aragon-circular-desde-marcilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Boyas del Campo a río Aragón – circular desde Marcilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 9.2 miles (14.8 km) long. This moderate route typically takes around 3 hours and 46 minutes to complete.

Q: How can I find more detailed information about specific routes?
A: Each route mentioned, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103051433/barranco-del-rey-circular-desde-funes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Rey – circular desde Funes&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1106404277/atalaya-de-peralta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya de Peralta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, has a dedicated page on komoot where you can find detailed maps, elevation profiles, photos, and reviews from other hikers.
tips:
Beautiful view over the merging of rivers Arga and Aragón.

With historic background, in 1076 the king of Navarra Sancho Garcés IV of Pamplona was killed when pushed off the cliff by his brother Ramón.

waypoints.name: View of Barranco del Rey and the confluence of the Arga and Aragón rivers
